explanation: The requirement states that when the machine is turned on (i.e., upon entering StartUpMode), default parameter values must be loaded from a configuration file. The atomic proposition 'defaultParamsLoaded' is the only available flag for the successful loading of parameters. We infer that the best mapping is: upon StartUpMode, eventually the default parameters have to be loaded. This is captured by using 'upon StartUpMode', 'eventually satisfy defaultParamsLoaded'.
decision3_substring: shall be provided and loaded from a configuration file on the machine when it is turned on

explanation: The requirement states that when the machine is turned on (i.e., during Start-up Mode), a set of default parameter values must be loaded from a configuration file. Among the provided atomic propositions, 'StartUpMode' indicates the system is currently in Start-up Mode and 'defaultParamsLoaded' indicates that the default parameters were loaded. Thus, the property should express that whenever Start-up Mode is entered, eventually the default parameters are loaded from the configuration file, reflecting the loading of defaults at system start. This is captured by: whenever StartUpMode, eventually satisfy defaultParamsLoaded.
decision3_substring: A set of default values for all parameters shall be provided and loaded from a configuration file on the machine when it is turned on.

explanation: The input requirement specifies that when the machine is turned on (i.e., at Start-up Mode), default parameter values must be loaded from a configuration file. Among the atomic propositions, 'StartUpMode' denotes that the system is in start-up, and 'defaultParamsLoaded' indicates that the default parameters have been successfully loaded. Thus, the property is best captured by stating that 'whenever StartUpMode, eventually satisfy defaultParamsLoaded', which ensures that for every start-up event, the system eventually loads the default parameters as required.
decision3_substring: default values for all parameters shall be provided and loaded from a configuration file on the machine when it is turned on.

explanation: The requirement states that, on start-up (when the machine is turned on), a set of default parameter values must be loaded from a configuration file. Based on the provided atomic propositions, 'StartUpMode' indicates the machine is undergoing its start-up procedure, and 'defaultParamsLoaded' indicates that the default parameter values have been loaded. The translation uses the template: whenever StartUpMode, eventually satisfy defaultParamsLoaded, expressing that, every time the system enters Start-up Mode, it is required that at some subsequent point the default parameters are loaded. This directly captures the intent of the requirement - to ensure that start-up always leads to the default parameters being loaded.

explanation: The requirement states that default parameter values must be loaded from a config file when the machine is turned on (i.e., at start-up). Given the provided atomic propositions, 'StartUpMode' indicates the system is in start-up, and 'defaultParamsLoaded' indicates the default parameters are loaded. Therefore, the structured NL expresses that whenever the system enters start-up mode, it must eventually load the default parameters. This captures the intent that the loading action is mandatory at each start-up event. The part about a configuration file is not captured as no proposition details file selection; we focus on loading the default values as per available atomic propositions.
decision3_substring: shall be provided and loaded from a configuration file on the machine when it is turned on

explanation: The original requirement states that when the machine is turned on (i.e., in Start-up Mode), a set of default values for all parameters must be provided and loaded from a configuration file. This intent is captured by specifying that 'whenever StartUpMode' holds, then 'defaultParamsLoaded' must eventually become true. 'StartUpMode' indicates that the system is in the Start-up Mode, and 'defaultParamsLoaded' denotes that the default parameters have been loaded. The structured natural language thus ensures that each time the system enters Start-up Mode, the configuration with the default parameters will be loaded at some point, reflecting the original requirement's intent.

'StartUpMode' indicates that the system is in the start-up phase, and 'defaultParamsLoaded' indicates that the default parameters have been loaded. The property should ensure that, whenever (or upon) entering StartUpMode, the default parameters are eventually loaded. This is best captured with a structured property: whenever StartUpMode, eventually satisfy defaultParamsLoaded, which matches the intent that the parameters are loaded during the start-up phase.

explanation: The requirement specifies that whenever the system enters Start-up Mode (which is handled by the StartUpMode atomic proposition), the default parameters must be loaded from a configuration file onto the machine. The closest available atomic proposition indicating the load of default parameters is 'defaultParamsLoaded'. The intent is captured even though the original states that it shall be loaded (eventually) on power on (i.e., start-up).

explanation: The requirement specifies that upon start-up (when the machine is turned on), default values for all parameters must be loaded from a configuration file. In the provided atomic propositions, StartUpMode indicates the system is in start-up, and defaultParamsLoaded indicates the default parameters were loaded. Therefore, whenever the StartUpMode condition holds (the system is starting up), defaultParamsLoaded must eventually occur (the parameters are indeed loaded from configuration). This structured NL property captures the intent of the input natural language by linking the startup event with the necessity of loading default parameters.
decision3_substring: default values for all parameters shall be provided and loaded from a configuration file on the machine when it is turned on.

explanation: The natural language requirement specifies that when the machine is turned on (i.e., at 'Start-up Mode'), a set of default values for all parameters needs to be loaded from a configuration file. Out of the available atomic propositions, 'StartUpMode' best indicates the system being in start-up mode, and 'defaultParamsLoaded' denotes that the default parameter values have been loaded. The most faithful structured NL translation uses the template: 'whenever bool_exp1, eventually satisfy bool_exp2', i.e., whenever the device enters Start-up Mode, eventually the default parameters must be loaded. This captures the intent because it ensures that at every entry to Start-up Mode, the loading of the default parameters happens (even if there might be some initialization actions first). No duration is specified, so timing is not included here.
decision1_substring: when it is turned on. Start-up Mode
decision3_substring: shall be provided and loaded from a configuration file on the machine when it is turned on.
